prompt: A master tailor's dim workshop. A single mannequin stands under a warm overhead bulb wearing a bespoke suit that has been tailored so precisely to one specific absent body that it looks grotesque in isolation — one shoulder is raised higher, one lapel twisted inward, extra fabric puckering at odd pressure points, the hem cut unevenly to match a particular person's uneven gait. The suit memorized one body perfectly and fits no general human shape. Scattered around are hundreds of discarded measurement notes pinned to the walls. Photorealistic, shallow depth of field, cinematic warm lighting. No text. No labels. No diagrams. The suit is the only subject.
metaphor: A suit tailored to every quirk of one specific body becomes unwearable by any other — memorization without generalization made tangible and physical.
